_readme: Gauge provenance: the human audit trail behind aliases.json.
Each entry under `gauges` is keyed `source:site` (lookupGauge's gaugeKey) and
records that the owner personally verified this station is the correct,
commonly used gauge for the run(s) that reference it. The machine can prove a
gauge is alive and fresh (src/canarySweep.ts); only a paddler can prove it is
the RIGHT gauge. test/provenance.test.ts enforces coverage via an inverted
ratchet: lower MAX_UNAUDITED there as entries are added; 0 = fully enforcing.
`rituals` records the seasonal real-device tests: a real InReach round trip
pre-season and mid-season, the only check that exercises the Garmin POST.

usgs:12359800.notes: SF Flathead ab Twin Creek — the free-flowing wilderness reach (Youngs Creek/Bob Marshall multiday + Meadow Creek Gorge). AW quotes this gauge for both sections. NOT 12362500 (Columbia Falls), which is Hungry Horse dam outflow on a different reach. Live-verified 5,340 cfs / 8.63 ft at add time.
